tldr: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen announced her candidacy for the 2027 presidential election, despite an appeals court upholding her conviction for misusing European Parliament funds which includes the requirement of wearing an ankle tag.

Hungary's public media outlets, including Kossuth radio and M1 TV channels, have suspended broadcasting in a move the new prime minister, Péter Magyar, described as the "end of propaganda broadcasts" following Viktor Orbán's ousting.
During the NATO summit, former US President Donald Trump reiterated his proposal for the US to acquire Greenland and threatened to remove all American forces from Europe.

market.why: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both closed higher on July 6. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite saw a significant rise, potentially fueled by developments in artificial intelligence from major companies like Microsoft and Meta, and projections that Elon Musk's Terafab could boost the chip sector. This broader tech optimism appears to have outweighed a sell-off in some chip stocks following Samsung's earnings.

yield_10y.why: The 10-year Treasury yield increased on July 7, as bond traders evaluated the potential for future Federal Reserve rate adjustments. This anticipation is influenced by upcoming economic data, including a Fed gauge of wage growth and a consumer inflation reading, with the minutes from the Fed's June meeting also expected to offer insights into monetary policy.

vix.why: The VIX decreased on July 6, signaling a reduction in anticipated market volatility. No specific reasons for this decline were explicitly detailed in the provided articles.

tech.summary: Anthropic is expanding the availability of its Claude Cowork AI platform to mobile and web, initially for Max subscribers, marking its first accessibility beyond the desktop app.
Meta has launched Muse Image, its first AI model designed for image creation, as part of its strategy to attract creators and advertisers to its platforms.
Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek is reportedly planning to develop its own chips, aiming to reduce its reliance on Nvidia and Huawei and establish independent production.

world.summary: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen confirmed her intention to run in the 2027 presidential election, despite an appeals court upholding her conviction for misusing European Parliament funds, which requires her to wear an ankle tag for a year.

Hungary's public media outlets, including Kossuth radio and M1 TV channels, have suspended broadcasting, a move described by the new prime minister, Péter Magyar, as the "end of propaganda broadcasts" following Viktor Orbán's ousting.
During the NATO summit, former US President Donald Trump renewed his call for the US to acquire Greenland and threatened to withdraw all American troops from Europe, reiterating his prior demands.
